# Bank of Namibia Determination PSD-TEST (Synthetic Test Document)

## Part 1: Customer Due Diligence Requirements

Section 4.1: Every payment service provider shall verify the identity of a
customer before establishing a business relationship, in accordance with the
Financial Intelligence Act. The provider shall obtain the full name, date of
birth, national identity number, and residential address of the customer.

Section 4.2: Payment service providers shall conduct enhanced due diligence
for cross-border transactions exceeding N$ 100,000, including verification
of the source of funds and the purpose of the transaction.

## Part 2: Reporting Obligations

Section 5.1: Providers shall submit a monthly transaction report to the Bank
of Namibia within 15 business days of month end, covering transaction volumes,
values, and currency composition for all payment streams.

Section 5.2: Suspicious transaction reports shall be filed with the Financial
Intelligence Centre within 3 business days of detection, in the format
prescribed under the Financial Intelligence Act.

## Part 3: Interoperability

Section 6.1: E-money issuers shall ensure interoperability with the national
payment switch within 12 months of licensing. Interoperability includes both
person-to-person transfers and merchant payment acceptance across all
licensed wallet providers in Namibia.
